methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $157M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 13 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $300M - $9.2B.

private.name: Hidden Level
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series C), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
BRINC Drones, a Series C defense and security drone manufacturer founded by Blake Resnick, has raised $157M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 13 same-sector and adjacent-stage comparables, comprising 2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Saronic, and Shield AI. The peer median valuation across this set is $1.2B, with a range spanning $300M to $9.2B.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses the $157M total raised as a proxy and applies a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C-to-next-stage step-ups, yielding $267M. The peer-median method anchors to the $1.2B median across the 13 comparable companies at the same or adjacent stage. The wide peer range reflects sector heterogeneity; sampled peers include Saronic at $9.2B (Series D), Chaos Industries at $4.5B (Series D), and Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, and Harmattan AI each valued around $1.4–$1.5B (Series B).

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $227M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $758M. Key uncertainties include the appropriate stage-adjustment multiplier for BRINC's specific market position, customer concentration, and regulatory risk in the defense sector—factors that drive wide dispersion within the peer set.
